name: Storming of the Bastille
summary: Revolutionaries in Paris stormed the Bastille prison, symbolizing the uprising against royal authority and marking a key moment in the French Revolution.

name: Estates-General Convenes
summary: King Louis XVI summoned the Estates-General to address France's financial crisis, setting the stage for revolutionary political change.

name: Tennis Court Oath
summary: Members of the Third Estate pledged to create a constitution, forming the National Assembly and challenging royal authority.

name: Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen
summary: France adopted a foundational document asserting liberty, equality, and individual rights, influencing democratic movements worldwide.

name: George Washington Inaugurated as First U.S. President
summary: George Washington took office as the first President of the United States, establishing precedents for the new nation's government.

name: Fletcher Christian Leads the Mutiny on the Bounty
summary: Crew members of HMS Bounty mutinied against Captain William Bligh, one of the most famous maritime rebellions in history.

name: Catherine the Great Strengthens Russian Administration
summary: Catherine the Great continued reforms and governance efforts, maintaining Russia's status as a major European power.
